blocks.text: Demand is steady but capacity discipline matters: 1.00 book-to-bill on $8.8M Q2 revenue.
Backlog ended at $8.6M, down from the prior quarter, while win rate was 36%.
Priority: protect the riskiest accounts while using capacity selectively on high-fit growth.
Verdict: Q2 2026 is a healthy but capacity-sensitive quarter, with demand slightly ahead of shipments and account risk requiring follow-up.
Evidence: revenue was $8.8M, bookings were $8.4M, book-to-bill was 1.00, and backlog ended at $8.6M.
Implication: leadership should protect Clearfork Turbine Works, prioritize high-fit work, and avoid filling constrained capacity with low-fit demand.

blocks.text: Capability gaps: assembly. Production constraints to clear before quoting: inspection queue, material availability, programmer availability. Be honest about any gap that requires a partner, qualification work, or schedule buffer.
Current capacity context shows 715 available 5-axis hours and 495 available turning hours in the next 30 days. The first quoted lead time is 24 days, with status "selective_capacity".

Verdict: pursue-with-caution. Deciding factor: fit or capacity is workable but constraints must be cleared before committing. Next step: qualify drawings, materials, certifications, timing, and whether constrained work should be pursued directly, partnered, or declined.

title: Sales Pitch - Gulf Coast Propulsion
confidenceReason: High: account need, public trigger, fit, and capacity context are available.

blocks.text: Gulf Coast Propulsion is balancing new production demand with qualified supplier capacity.

sections.heading: What BTX Does for Companies Like Them

blocks.text: BTX Precision helps aerospace and defense manufacturers move qualified machined work through certified outside capacity. For Gulf Coast Propulsion, the most relevant fit is precision machining, ITAR, aluminum. We keep the conversation practical: prints, materials, timing, and what can be supported without overpromising.
BTX brings AS9100 and ITAR discipline, 5-axis machining capacity, build-to-print execution, and delivery follow-through for production teams that need dependable support.

Recent public evidence points to timing: Gulf Coast Propulsion posted a surge of manufacturing and supply-chain openings. That makes now a useful moment to compare what they need against where BTX may help.

Ask Alyssa Wright for a 20-minute fit call with one current print package or work statement, then confirm materials, certifications, delivery window, and whether BTX Dallas Main has available 5-axis capacity with quoted lead time of 24 days.

blocks.text: David Mendez, VP Supply Chain at Desert Sky Machining.
Desert Sky Machining production-capacity conversation

Hi David,

I noticed Desert Sky Machining was awarded a $0.2M government production contract. BTX Precision supports aerospace and defense teams with precision machining, 5-axis machining, and certified build-to-print work when programs need qualified outside capacity. Could we schedule 20 minutes next week to see whether there is a useful fit?

Alyssa Hart
Chief Revenue Officer, BTX Precision
